title: Jak (Nautolan)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jak}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Jak_Legacy_14.jpg]] |name=Jak |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]][[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{CSWECite|II|135|Jak (1)}} |pronouns=He/him''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |skin=Green |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Rik's Cantina/Legends|Rik's Cantina]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Come see me home, Jak.|Queen Jool|[[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]]}} '''Jak''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] who grew up in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]], where he befriended the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Cade Skywalker]], a frequent visitor of the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] underlevels. By [[137 ABY]], Jak had become the personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Jool]] and worked as an enforcer at [[Rik's Cantina/Legends|Rik's Cantina]], which she owned. His loyalty to Jool was unflinching, and he would rid the establishment of anyone who caused her trouble if she commanded it. Skywalker visited the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in 137 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] but was soon after taken captive by [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] ruling [[One Sith|Sith]]; Jak later witnessed a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] that Skywalker had placed inside of his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. After the hologram concluded, a group of Skywalker's allies began planning a rescue of him from the clutches of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], but Jak left the mission in their hands and exited the cantina. ==Biography== {{Quote|Jak.''"
"''Cade.|Cade Skywalker and Jak nonchalantly greet each other|}} The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] Jak lived in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] of [[127 ABY|127]]–[[130 ABY]].[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] During his youth, Jak often encountered and eventually befriended the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Cade Skywalker]], who visited the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] underlevels to escape from his rigorous [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]]. By [[137 ABY]], Jak was working as the personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Jool]], the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] owner of [[Rik's Cantina/Legends|Rik's Cantina]], a well-known [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] and meeting place in the Coruscant underworld. Jak also served as the cantina's enforcer and had become a regular fixture in the establishment to the point that his presence was often nearly forgotten by its denizens. In 137 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], Skywalker returned to the cantina. Jak was surprised to see his old friend, as the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] had been [[Massacre at Ossus|decimated]] by [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith|Sith Order]] seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, and Krayt had [[Attack on Emperor Fel|usurped]] the galactic throne on Coruscant.[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] Nevertheless, the two nonchalantly greeted each other at the door. Skywalker, who was on his way to the [[Temple of the Sith]] to rescue the captive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]], left the access codes to his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], with Jool in case he should not return. His co-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Jariah Syn]] and [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Deliah Blue]] arrived shortly thereafter and retrieved the codes. Jak and Jool joined them inside the cantina as they watched a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] Skywalker had placed inside of his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], telling them not to attempt to rescue him. Soon to arrive were the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]] and [[Kee]], who had their [[Grinning Liar|own reasons]] for wanting Skywalker rescued, and [[Morrigan Corde]], the [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of Skywalker. At Jool's word, Jak escorted her to her home while the motley crew remained in the bar and discussed how they could best retrieve Skywalker from the temple. ==Personality and traits== [[File:Hegongetsome.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Jak with Queen Jool]] Jak had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and green skin. As Jool's bodyguard, Jak was completely loyal to the Hutt. His first priority was to protect her, and he dealt with troublemakers in Rik's Cantina at her command by tossing them out the bar's front door. The strong and silent type, Jak's presence was often nearly forgotten by patrons of the bar as he kept watch over the entire room; he was particularly vigilant when it was visited by someone he did not recognize. Despite being surprised to see Cade Skywalker in 137 ABY, Jak gave his old friend only a one-word greeting when Skywalker arrived at the cantina. He was content in the knowledge that he was hired muscle who was not required to think very much, and had no objections to not participating in the rescue of Skywalker from the Temple of the Sith. ==Equipment== While on Coruscant, Jak wore brown [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]. ==Behind the scenes== Jak first appeared in the [[Legacy (2006) 14|fourteenth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]], pencilled by Duursema, and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[July 11]], [[2007]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54386/star_wars_legacy_2006_14|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #14}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jak (Nautolan)}} *[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jak (Nautolaner) |es=Jak (nautolano) |pl=Jak (Nautolanin) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Jak}} [[Category:Bodyguards]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Nautolans]]
